commit.message:
Fix 4 audit issues in TRT VAE encoder implementation

- hires_transform: zero tile dims when TRT covers full image to prevent
  feeding wrong-sized tiles to a static engine
- _check_model_ready: treat vae_enc as optional so missing encoder ONNX
  doesn't block all TRT for the model
- _do_export: load VAE once and reuse for both decoder and encoder export
- _vae_encode: add guard for both vae and trt_runner being None

commit.message:
Fix migration 015: DROP+CREATE view instead of CREATE OR REPLACE

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW cannot insert a column in the middle of an
existing view definition (PostgreSQL restriction). This caused migration
015 to fail with a column-naming error, preventing the backend container
from starting (502 error).

Fix: use DROP VIEW IF EXISTS + CREATE VIEW (same pattern as migration 012).
community_id is added at the end of the column list to avoid any ordering
constraint for future CREATE OR REPLACE changes.

Fix prevNcMap direction bug — NC messages are direction='received' in DB

Non-connected employee messages arrive tagged as direction='received'
(they are inbound to team WhatsApp accounts). The prevNcMap block was
incorrectly checking for 'sent', meaning NC employees always had 0 sent
count and empty chats set in previous period → all NC employee deltas
showed 'No comparison' in Team Performance table.

Fix mirrors current-period ncMap logic: count direction='received'
messages as NC employee 'sent' activity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix /restart to work with tsx watch (npm run dev)

Spawn a detached process that touches src/index.ts after exit,
triggering tsx watch to restart the bot. Under systemd the touch
is harmless since Restart=always handles it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: player profile signals — bounce_back_over, over_streak_reversion, under_after_streak filter

Session 418: Three new player-profile-based signal/filter additions:

1. bounce_back_over (SHADOW): OVER after bad miss (<70% of line) + AWAY game.
   56.2% raw over rate (N=379), 60%+ with model confirmation. Bounce-back
   completely disappears at home (47.8%).

2. over_streak_reversion_under (SHADOW): UNDER after 4+ overs in last 5 games.
   56% UNDER rate (N=366). Progressive mean reversion.

3. under_after_streak (ACTIVE filter): Blocks UNDER picks on players with 3+
   consecutive unders at edge < 5.0. Model blind spot — these hit only 44.7%
   (N=515). Edge >= 5.0 guard preserves high-conviction UNDER calls.

Infrastructure: Added prev_game_context CTE to supplemental_data.py for
previous game stats (points, line, FG%, ratio). Added prop_over_streak and
over_rate_last_10 from feature store.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(phase-5a): implement family dashboard backend API endpoints

Adds family protection API handlers for retrieving family data and joining families:

🔌 API Endpoints:
  • GET /api/v1/family/dashboard - Retrieve family members and recent threats
  • POST /api/v1/family/join - Join a family using invite code
  • OPTIONS /api/v1/family/* - CORS preflight support

📊 Features:
  • Extract user ID from JWT Bearer token
  • Query family metadata, members, and threats from DynamoDB
  • Support invite code-based family joining
  • Update user profile with familyId upon joining
  • Graceful error handling for missing families, invalid codes, etc.

🔐 Authentication:
  • Bearer token validation from Authorization header
  • JWT payload extraction (sub claim for user ID)
  • Token format validation and error responses

💾 DynamoDB Integration:
  • Uses composite key pattern (PK/SK)
  • Queries family records by ID
  • Scans for family by invite code
  • Updates user profiles with family association

✅ Response Format:
  • Standardized success/error JSON responses
  • CORS headers on all responses
  • Proper HTTP status codes (200, 400, 401, 404, 500)
  • Error codes and messages for debugging

📝 Unit Tests:
  • 13 test scenarios covering all endpoints
  • Mock DynamoDB table interactions
  • JWT token generation and validation
  • Error handling scenarios (missing auth, invalid code, etc.)
  • Response format validation

Code Quality:
  • 280 lines of production-ready code
  • Full docstrings for all functions
  • Error logging for debugging
  • Type-consistent parameter handling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix sign-in: unify Firebase Auth across all pages via cart.js

Each page was creating its own named Firebase app (public-shop,
public-product, shir-cart, etc.) with independent auth persistence.
Signing in on one app instance didn't propagate to others, so the
popup completed but nothing visibly changed.

Now cart.js uses the default Firebase app and owns all auth:
siteSignIn, siteSignOut, ensureCustomerAccount, and nav UI updates.
Per-page auth scripts removed (or simplified to page-specific UI only).

Fix all 50 eslint errors across source and test files

Resolve trailing commas, brace style, max-statements-per-line,

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Uninstall torchcodec before audio download to prevent ABI crash

torchcodec is pre-installed in the container but has ABI mismatch with
the container's PyTorch. HF datasets tries to use it for audio decoding
and crashes with SIGABRT. Explicitly uninstall it so HF falls back to
soundfile automatically.

Body tab retains separate Pitch and Amp envelope modes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add blog post: The Right Detail, Not More Detail (#14)

* Add blog post: More Wonder and Woe with AI

Field report on an AI-assisted V2 API build that took 5 phases instead of 3
because the domain model wasn't mapped before coding. References Eric Evans'
Domain-Driven Design (2003) on Ubiquitous Language. Includes LinkedIn post.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Dr77GxabL7DRxyHHHfKYz

* Rewrite post: shift focus to writing things down and knowledge asymmetry

Tighten to ~100 lines. Reframe takeaway from "human should have known"
to "what's obvious to one is invisible to the other — write it down."
Emphasize one clear upfront plan over iterative discovery.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Dr77GxabL7DRxyHHHfKYz

* Correct narrative: domain knowledge was discussed, not trapped

The AI asked the right question and got the right answer — the lossy
step was conversation-to-plan, not brain-to-conversation. Reframe
around writing down the important stuff, not micromanaging every line.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Dr77GxabL7DRxyHHHfKYz

* Refine narrative: right detail vs more detail, add Dr. John reference

The knowledge wasn't missing — it was lost in the iterative process of
writing increasingly detailed plans. "More detail is not better than
the right detail." Add Dr. John footnote for "Right Place Wrong Time."

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Dr77GxabL7DRxyHHHfKYz

* Remove LinkedIn draft from post

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Dr77GxabL7DRxyHHHfKYz

* Rewrite LinkedIn draft: stronger hook, add throughput-to-nulls contrast

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Dr77GxabL7DRxyHHHfKYz

* Remove LinkedIn draft from blog post

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Dr77GxabL7DRxyHHHfKYz

* improve caption

* Rewrite The Wonder section: focus on PRD corruption through phasing

The original paragraph read as a straightforward success story. The
rewrite centers the real thesis — the feeling of speed was the trap,
and each phase translation lost foundational context while gaining
implementation detail.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Dr77GxabL7DRxyHHHfKYz

* Rewrite post: The Right Detail, Not More Detail

Complete rewrite focusing on signal decay in AI-generated artifacts.
Core thesis: AI documents derived from AI documents lose domain context
fast. One clear domain map plus validation agents beats five derivative
phase plans. References Eric Evans, Dr. John, Homer Simpson.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Dr77GxabL7DRxyHHHfKYz

* final version

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Show trade confirmation immediately, don't block on UI refresh

Success message was hidden behind await updateUI() which fetches
prices and can take seconds. Now shows confirmation with trade details
right after save, bumps modal timeout to 2s for readability, and runs
UI refresh in background. Both buy and sell paths fixed.

Replace markdown histograms with publication-quality visualizations

Replace text-based histogram tables with proper stacked bar chart
visualizations showing sample size distributions.

Added visualizations:
- fig1: Sample size distribution by technology (stacked bars, 19 techs)
- fig2: Sample size distribution by material (stacked bars, top 20)
- fig3: Overall distribution (histogram with summary statistics)

Features:
- 300 DPI publication-quality PNG images
- Color-coded by sample size bin (red→purple gradient)
- Stacked bars show composition within each tech/material
- Summary statistics box on overall distribution
- Clear legends and labeled axes

Removed:
- table9/table10 markdown files (replaced by proper visualizations)

The stacked bar charts make it easy to see:
- Which technologies have balanced vs sparse data
- Which materials have the broadest coverage
- Overall dataset composition across sample size bins

fix: grading bugs — game_datetime_utc missing column, yaml lazy import

subset_grading_processor referenced game_datetime_utc which doesn't exist
in nba_schedule. shared/validation/__init__.py eagerly imported yaml via
scraper_config_validator, crashing grading CF. Fixed with lazy __getattr__.

feat: player deep dive analysis tool and signal discovery

9-module reusable player analysis tool (bin/analysis/player_deep_dive.py).
Curry deep dive revealed 10 exploitable patterns from player scoring
distributions: bounce-back is AWAY-only (56.2%, N=379), model blind spot
on streak UNDER calls (44.7%, N=515), volatile+high edge = 61.9% (N=3K).
